summary: Prior blocking issues were addressed in the plan text; residual warning is to rerun plan review after Phases 1 and 2 are actually applied.

context.phase_ordering_assumption: Phase 3 will be implemented after Phase 1 and Phase 2 local checkpoints; fixes preserve that future-state assumption.
context.dirty_worktree_note: The target plan and overview were already modified before this review cycle; this sidecar records the post-fix readiness state of the working-tree version.

warnings.issue: Phase 3 still depends on future Phase 1 and Phase 2 edits, so exact line numbers and direct call-site inventories must be rechecked during execution.
warnings.resolution: Before executing Task 3.7, rerun the listed rg inventories and hard gate against the live post-Phase-2 tree.

resolved_findings.resolution: Predicate-boundary RunResult snippets now remove the stray closing parens and use COMPLETED_WITH_FAILURES for impossible subset shapes so the new subset guard is the intended RED signal.
Phase 3 file inventory and atomic git add block now include web sessions, session tests, the operator migration stub, and direct SinkExecutor.write() test fallout.
SinkExecutor.write() migration now requires an all-tree rg inventory and names the direct unit/integration test files that must be updated or verified.
Task 3.5a now adds OPERATOR_COMPLETION_RUN_STATUS_VALUES and requires landscape_run_id for completed, completed_with_failures, and empty.
Task 3.6 now specifies same-file helper contracts for live and interrupt/resume scenarios instead of leaving placeholder helper calls.
Task 3.1 and 3.2 now require DB-backed token-outcome assertions for durable outcome/path/completed evidence.
Task 3.3 now keeps COALESCED as producer-carried coalesce evidence and requires token.join_group_id to be preserved into sink-side recording.

Frontend terminal-status handling now uses a type guard, Phase 3/Phase 5 test-gate language is synchronized with the overview, policy gates are explicit, diversion partial-failure timing is documented, discard prose uses two-axis terms, and helper integrity tests are setup verification rather than behaviour RED tests.

recommendations: Execute the phase task-by-task after Phases 1 and 2 have been applied locally.
Treat the Task 3.7 hard gate as mandatory before the first Stage 2/3 commit.
Rerun plan review if the Phase 1 or Phase 2 implementation diverges from the plan assumptions.
